item.title: Xi Hosts Taiwan Opposition Leader to Draw Island Closer to China
item.description: Ahead of a visit by President Trump , the mainland’s campaign to absorb Taiwan and muffle U.S. influence advances.

item.title: Middle East Energy Shock Snaps China’s Deflationary Streak
item.description: Factory-gate prices in the world’s second-largest economy rose for the first time in more than three years.

item.title: Trump Once Talked Tough With China. Now He’s Playing Nice.
item.description: The administration has quietly scrapped its previous playbook, adopting a more conciliatory approach to Beijing

item.title: China Gains Favor With Trump by Dipping Into Iran Diplomacy
item.description: Beijing hopes that nudging Tehran into a cease-fire buys goodwill for May summit between the president and Xi Jinping.

item.title: Qingming and the Weight of Memory
item.description: Plus, a life consumed by the internal and external conflicts that forged modern China.

item.title: China Creates New Aviation Mystery With Offshore Warning Zones
item.description: Beijing has reserved swaths of airspace for 40 days, suggesting possible military activity ahead.

item.title: Xi Ousts Politburo Member in Deepening Purge of China’s Top Ranks
item.description: A former party chief of the Xinjiang region became the third member of the elite body to be removed in recent months.

item.title: As China Encroaches, Even New Zealand Is Getting Serious About Its Military
item.description: Billions will be spent in the coming years—including on missiles, drones and base upgrades—as the remote country squares up to a widening threat.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.
